name: Tulsi
desc: Boost immunity
usage: Leaves consumed as tea or raw to boost immunity, treat cold, cough, and respiratory infections.

name: Neem
desc: Good for skin
usage: Leaves paste applied to skin for acne, eczema, and infections. Bark used for dental hygiene.
benefits: Antibacterial, antifungal, anti-inflammatory, blood purifier, insecticidal

name: Turmeric
desc: Anti inflammatory
usage: Rhizome powder added to food or milk (golden milk). Used topically for wound healing.
benefits: Anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antimicrobial, hepatoprotective, anticancer properties

name: Aloe Vera
desc: Good for skin
usage: Gel extracted from leaves applied topically for burns, sunburn, moisturization, and wound healing.
benefits: Skin soothing, anti-inflammatory, wound healing, laxative (latex), antimicrobial

name: Ginger
desc: Helps in digestion and cold
usage: Rhizome used in teas, cooking, and supplements. Relieves nausea, indigestion, and cold symptoms.
benefits: Digestive stimulant, anti-nausea, anti-inflammatory, analgesic, antioxidant

name: Ashwagandha
desc: Reduces stress and improves strength
usage: Root powder consumed with milk or as capsules. Reduces cortisol levels and enhances endurance.

name: Mint
desc: Good for stomach and freshness
usage: Leaves used in teas, chutneys, and mouth fresheners. Relieves indigestion and IBS symptoms.
benefits: Digestive, carminative, antimicrobial, analgesic, cooling, refreshing

name: Giloy
desc: Improves immunity and fights fever
usage: Stem juice or powder taken internally. Used in Ayurveda for fever, dengue, and immune support.

name: Brahmi
desc: Improves memory and brain power
usage: Leaves taken as juice, powder or capsules. Used for cognitive enhancement and stress relief.
benefits: Nootropic, memory enhancer, anxiolytic, neuroprotective, adaptogenic

name: Lemon
desc: Rich in vitamin C and good for skin
usage: Juice applied topically for skin brightening. Consumed in water for detox and immune support.
benefits: Antioxidant, vitamin C source, skin brightening, antimicrobial, digestive aid

name: Ran Bhendi
desc: A wild okra species used in folk medicine across India.
usage: Leaves and seeds used as poultice for skin diseases; decoction taken for fever and inflammation.

name: Jangli Bhindi
desc: A wild relative of okra used in traditional Indian medicine.
usage: Leaves and flowers consumed as vegetables or herbal teas; used for digestive complaints and wounds.
benefits: Digestive, anti-inflammatory, wound healing, mild laxative

name: Muskdana
desc: Musk mallow with aromatic seeds used in Ayurvedic medicine.
usage: Seeds used as nervine tonic and carminative; oil used in perfumery and topically for skin.
benefits: Antispasmodic, carminative, nervine tonic, aphrodisiac, antimicrobial

name: Talish patra
desc: Himalayan silver fir used in Ayurvedic and Tibetan medicine.
usage: Bark and leaves used as decoction for treating cough, asthma, and bronchitis.

name: Ulatkambal
desc: Devil's cotton, used in traditional Indian medicine for gynecological and skin conditions.
usage: Root bark used for menstrual disorders; leaves applied topically for skin problems.

name: Ratti
desc: Rosary pea used cautiously in Ayurveda; seeds are toxic in large doses.
usage: Leaves used for skin diseases and hair loss; root used as a tonic in very small quantities.
benefits: Antibacterial, antifungal, hair tonic, anti-inflammatory (use with caution)

name: Barakanghi
desc: A hairy mallow species used in folk medicine for immune support.
usage: Leaves used as diuretic and antipyretic; seeds used for treating cough and urinary disorders.

name: Kanghi
desc: Indian mallow used widely in Ayurvedic medicine for respiratory conditions.
usage: Root and bark decoction for cough, bronchitis; leaves applied for joint pain.

name: Kakrai
desc: A fibrous mallow species used in traditional Indian medicine.
usage: Used for treating skin diseases, fevers, and rheumatic conditions in folk medicine.

name: Indian Mallow
desc: Velvet leaf or Indian mallow used as a traditional digestive and skin remedy.
usage: Seeds used for dysentery and diarrhea; leaves as poultice for skin inflammation.

name: Vilayati kikar
desc: Sweet acacia used in perfumery and traditional medicine.
usage: Bark and pods used as astringent for throat infections; flowers used in perfumes and tonics.

name: Kikar
desc: A thorny acacia native to the arid regions of India and Pakistan.
usage: Gum and bark used for skin diseases, wound healing, and as an astringent.

name: Safed kikar
desc: White-barked acacia used in traditional Indian medicine.
usage: Bark decoction used for diarrhea and dysentery; gum used as demulcent for stomach ailments.

name: Phulai
desc: A thorny shrub from northwestern India used in folk medicine.
usage: Bark decoction used as tonic and for treating fever; leaves as fodder and in herbal preparations.

name: Babula
desc: Egyptian thorn acacia widely used in Ayurveda for dental and skin care.
usage: Bark used for toothache, gingivitis; gum as astringent; pods for diarrhea.
benefits: Dental astringent, antimicrobial, antidiarrheal, wound healing, anti-inflammatory

name: Aila
desc: Climbing wattle used in Southeast Asian and Indian folk medicine.
usage: Leaves and bark used for treating skin infections and as general immunity tonic.

name: Kochi
desc: A thorny acacia used in traditional South Asian medicine for skin conditions.
usage: Bark and leaf extracts applied for skin infections, rashes, and as astringent.

name: Goradio-babul
desc: Senegalia senegal, source of gum arabic, used for respiratory and digestive ailments.
usage: Gum dissolved in water taken for sore throat, cough, and as expectorant.
benefits: Expectorant, demulcent, anti-inflammatory, soothing for mucous membranes

name: Kuppi
desc: Indian acalypha used in Siddha and Ayurvedic medicine for respiratory disorders.
usage: Leaves used as expectorant for bronchitis and asthma; juice applied for skin diseases.

name: Candle Nut Tree
desc: Kukui nut tree, whose oil is used extensively in skin care.
usage: Nut oil applied topically for dry skin, eczema, and wound healing.

name: Alasyakampa
desc: A monopodial orchid used in traditional tribal medicine in India.
usage: Whole plant paste used for joint pain and immune support in folk remedies.

name: Hargoza
desc: Sea holly, a mangrove shrub used in coastal folk medicine in India.
usage: Root and leaf decoction used for asthma, paralysis, and as general tonic.

name: Kirayat
desc: King of bitters, a key herb in Ayurveda and traditional Chinese medicine.
usage: Leaves and stems used as bitter tonic for fever, liver disorders, and immune support.

name: Gandana
desc: Yarrow, a widely used medicinal herb in Europe and Asia.
usage: Aerial parts used as tea for fever, cold, wound healing, and digestive complaints.
benefits: Antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, astringent, wound healing

name: Jangli-jira
desc: A weed used in folk medicine across tropical Asia.
usage: Leaves used as poultice for wounds; decoction taken for cough and fever.

name: Latjira
desc: Prickly chaff flower, widely used in Ayurveda for respiratory and urinary conditions.
usage: Whole plant used for cough, asthma, kidney stones, and as diuretic.

name: Lal sag
desc: Red amaranth, a nutritious leafy vegetable used medicinally in India.
usage: Leaves eaten as vegetable; seeds used for anemia and to boost general immunity.

name: Kataili chaulai
desc: Spiny amaranth used in folk medicine for skin and liver conditions.
usage: Leaves used as poultice for skin boils; root decoction for snake bite and diuresis.

name: Atis
desc: Atis root, a Himalayan herb used carefully in Ayurveda for immune and digestive support.
usage: Processed root used for fever, digestive disorders, and as general tonic.
benefits: Antipyretic, digestive, immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory (processed use only)

name: Bikhima
desc: A Himalayan aconite species used in Ayurvedic medicine after detoxification.
usage: Processed root used for fever and as immunomodulatory tonic (must be detoxified before use).

name: Bach
desc: Sweet flag, a rhizomatous herb used extensively in Ayurveda and traditional medicine.
usage: Rhizome powder used for digestive disorders, memory enhancement, and as nervine tonic.
benefits: Nervine tonic, carminative, anti-inflammatory, nootropic, antimicrobial

name: Boro mankachu
desc: Giant taro used in tropical folk medicine for skin conditions.
usage: Leaves applied topically as anti-inflammatory poultice; stem used for fever.

name: Ghee-kunvar
desc: The true aloe vera used widely in skin care and Ayurveda.
usage: Gel extracted from leaves for burns, skin soothing, and oral consumption for digestive health.
benefits: Skin soothing, wound healing, anti-inflammatory, laxative, antidiabetic

name: Piyaz
desc: Onion, a widely used culinary and medicinal plant.
usage: Bulb consumed raw or cooked; juice applied to insect bites and skin infections.
benefits: Antibacterial, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protective, immunomodulatory

name: Bel
desc: Bael, a sacred fruit tree with excellent digestive properties.
usage: Ripe fruit pulp consumed for constipation; raw fruit for diarrhea and dysentery.

name: Kulanjan
desc: Greater galangal used in cooking and traditional medicine for digestion.
usage: Rhizome used for indigestion, nausea, and respiratory infections.
benefits: Carminative, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antifungal, digestive stimulant

name: Morang elaichi
desc: Bengal cardamom used as digestive spice and in Ayurvedic medicine.
usage: Seeds used for indigestion, flatulence, and nausea.

name: Bari elachi
desc: Black cardamom, a large cardamom used in cooking and medicine.
usage: Pods used for treating cough, throat infections, and as an expectorant.

name: Chatium
desc: Devil tree, used in Ayurveda and folk medicine as immunity tonic.
usage: Bark decoction used for fever, malaria, and as general tonic.

name: Kaju
desc: Cashew tree, whose bark and leaves are used medicinally.
usage: Bark tea used for diabetes management; leaf decoction for toothache and fever.

name: Toothpick plant
desc: Khella or toothpick weed used for respiratory and urinary conditions.
usage: Seeds used as bronchodilator for asthma; compound khellin extracted for kidney stones.

name: Gorak
desc: Baobab tree, the superfood tree of Africa, used widely for immune health.
usage: Fruit pulp consumed for vitamin C; bark used for fever and malaria.

name: Hansraj
desc: Maidenhair fern, used in Ayurveda and European medicine for respiratory conditions.
usage: Fronds made into syrup or tea for cough, bronchitis, and as expectorant.

name: Pangar
desc: Indian horse chestnut from the Himalayas used in folk medicine.
usage: Seeds used for joint pain and fever; oil applied topically for rheumatism.

name: Bans-keora
desc: Century plant, used in traditional medicine for digestive and skin disorders.
usage: Sap (aguamiel) used for digestive disorders; leaves used topically for wounds.

name: Uchunti
desc: Billy goat weed used in tropical folk medicine for skin and wound care.
usage: Leaves crushed and applied to wounds, skin infections, and burns.

name: Utis
desc: Nepalese alder, a tree used in folk medicine for skin and wound treatment.
usage: Bark decoction used for skin diseases and as astringent; leaves applied for wounds.

name: Arlu
desc: Indian tree of heaven, used in Ayurveda for respiratory and digestive issues.
usage: Bark decoction used for asthma, bronchitis, and fever.

name: Nilkanthi
desc: Bugle weed from the Himalayas used in traditional medicine for fever and immunity.
usage: Whole plant decoction used for fever, diabetes, and as general tonic.

name: Siris
desc: Woman's tongue tree, used in Ayurveda for respiratory and skin conditions.
usage: Bark decoction used for asthma, bronchitis; seeds for eye diseases and skin infections.

name: Bharbharra
desc: Camel thorn, a thorny shrub used in folk medicine for digestive and skin disorders.
usage: Aerial parts used as laxative and for treating skin diseases and hemorrhoids.

name: Harkakra
desc: Golden trumpet, an ornamental plant with antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties.
usage: Bark extract used for fever and parasitic infections; leaves for skin conditions.

name: Birkubat
desc: A small herb related to Kirayat, used in south Indian traditional medicine.
usage: Whole plant used for fever, liver disorders, and as immune tonic.

name: Aloysia
desc: Lemon verbena, an aromatic herb used for digestive and nervine complaints.
usage: Leaves brewed as tea for indigestion, flatulence, and anxiety.
benefits: Carminative, antispasmodic, nervine tonic, anti-inflammatory, digestive

name: Akola
desc: Sage-leaved alangium used in Ayurveda as tonic and antirheumatic.
usage: Bark and root decoction used for rheumatism, fever, and as general tonic.

name: Gorakhganja
desc: Mountain knotgrass used in Ayurveda for urinary and immune health.
usage: Whole plant decoction used for kidney stones, urinary infections, and as immune tonic.

name: Jonkmari
desc: Scarlet pimpernel used in folk medicine for liver and immune disorders.
usage: Aerial parts used for liver complaints, depression, and as blood purifier.

name: Ananas
desc: Pineapple, rich in bromelain enzyme for digestive and anti-inflammatory use.
usage: Fruit consumed fresh or as juice for digestion; bromelain used for inflammation.
benefits: Digestive enzyme (bromelain), anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, immune boosting

name: Jangli-angur
desc: A wild grape relative used in tribal medicine in India.
usage: Root decoction used for fever and as immunity tonic in folk remedies.

name: Gong
desc: Black galangal used in Northeast Indian cooking and folk medicine.
usage: Rhizome used as digestive spice and for stomach complaints.

name: Suranakanda
desc: Elephant foot yam used in Ayurveda for digestive and hepatic disorders.
usage: Corm consumed as vegetable and in Ayurvedic formulations for piles and digestive issues.

name: Kakamari
desc: Fish berry plant, used cautiously in Ayurveda for skin and nervous system disorders.
usage: Seeds used in very small quantities for skin diseases and as nervine; toxic in large doses.
benefits: Antimicrobial, nervine (use with extreme caution), skin healing

name: Jangli Mehndi
desc: Eared toothcup, a wetland herb used in folk medicine for skin conditions.
usage: Leaf paste applied to skin for fungal infections, rashes, and inflammation.
benefits: Antifungal, anti-inflammatory, wound healing, skin soothing

name: Dadmari
desc: Blistering ammannia used in folk medicine for skin diseases and herpes.
usage: Leaf paste applied externally for ringworm, eczema, and skin infections.

name: Couch grass
desc: A perennial grass widely used in European and Ayurvedic herbal medicine.
usage: Rhizome tea used for urinary tract infections and as immune tonic.

name: Perumkorattaikodi
scientific_name: Adenia wightiana (Wall. ex Wight & Arn.) Engl.
desc: A wild climber used in tribal medicine in South India.
usage: Root and leaf extracts used for immune support and skin conditions.

name: Silaras
desc: Rasamala tree, whose resin is used in traditional medicine for respiratory issues.
usage: Resin used as expectorant for bronchitis and cough; also used in incense.

name: Tudambo
desc: A laurel species used in tribal folk medicine in India.
usage: Bark and leaves used as immune tonic and for treating fevers.

name: Barigumchi
desc: Red sandalwood tree, seeds used in traditional medicine for inflammation.
usage: Seed decoction used for rheumatism, fever, and as anti-inflammatory.

name: Morpankhi
desc: Fan fern used in folk medicine for fever and general wellness.
usage: Fronds used as decoction for fever, cough, and as general tonic.

name: Mayurshikha
desc: Tailed maidenhair fern, known in Sanskrit as Mayurshikha.
usage: Fronds used for treating cough, asthma, fever, and hair-related disorders.

name: Kalijhant
desc: A maidenhair fern used in traditional medicine in India.
usage: Used for treating fevers, cough, and liver complaints in Ayurvedic and folk medicine.

name: Paowlay
desc: Known as Paowlay in Nepal. A small tree found in tropical forests.
usage: Bark and leaves used for treating fever, skin diseases, and digestive problems.

name: Vishaphala
desc: Baneberry, known as Vishaphala in Kannada. A toxic plant used carefully.
usage: Used in very small quantities for treating rheumatic pain and nervous disorders.

name: Ageli
desc: A Himalayan anemone found in alpine meadows, known as Ageli in Jaunsar.
usage: Used for treating skin diseases, rheumatic pains, and as a rubefacient in folk medicine.

name: Gandha priyangu
desc: Known as Yerraaduga in Telugu. A tree used in Ayurvedic formulations.
usage: Bark and flowers used for treating fever, skin diseases, and as an anti-inflammatory.

name: Chauli
desc: Also called Sauri. A forage legume with medicinal uses.
usage: Used for treating fever, skin diseases, and as a diuretic in traditional medicine.
